title: Identity Match Response (Provider → Router)
description: Provider-to-router shape of the Identity Match response. An identity agent returns eligibility, the per-package serve-window throttle, and — when it mints one — an ordered list of opaque TMPX value chunks. Names live on the publisher deployment side (see publisher-tmpx-config.json) and MUST NOT appear on the wire between provider and router; the provider owns ordering only. The router transforms this shape into the publisher-facing identity-match-response.json by keying the values under the emitting provider_id. Extension fields (ext, context) are intentionally omitted to prevent data leakage across the identity privacy boundary.

type.description: Message type discriminator for deserialization. Same const as the router→publisher variant so publisher-facing decoders can key off type before dispatching on shape.

request_id.description: Echoed request identifier from the identity match request.

eligible_package_ids.description: Package IDs the user is eligible for. Packages not listed are ineligible.

serve_window_sec.description: Per-package single-shot fcap window, in seconds. After serving the user one impression on each eligible package within this window, the publisher MUST re-query Identity Match before serving from those packages again. Not a router response cache TTL — it is a buyer-asserted serve throttle. Multi-impression frequency caps are handled separately by the buyer's impression tracker. Maximum 300.

tmpx_values.description: Ordered opaque TMPX chunks for this identity agent. Each entry is a URL-safe wire string the publisher substitutes verbatim into whatever ad-server slot its deployment configuration maps to this provider_id / ordinal — providers do NOT emit publisher-local names. Capped at 2 chunks in v1 to support multi-chunk TMPX (a single opaque token that exceeds one macro slot); the cap MAY rise without a shape change. Position within the array is the chunk's identity; the publisher's tmpx_macro_mapping (see publisher-tmpx-config.json) determines destination. Omitted when this provider mints no TMPX (e.g. no eligible packages).
